tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal odor, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria) further support renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. Low HbA1c and absence of infection markers are noted. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
